title: Pre-op respiratory risk documentation anesthesia
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

examination.text: Supports documenting pertinent medical records, patient interview, directed physical examination, airway evaluation, and lung and heart examination before elective anesthesia.
examination.rationale: The ASA advisory supports pertinent record review, respiratory history, directed airway and lung examination, and clinician-selected existing tests without generating a respiratory diagnosis or investigation.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports documenting pertinent medical records, patient interview, directed physical examination, airway evaluation, and lung and heart examination before elective anesthesia.

source.title: Practice Advisory for Preanesthesia Evaluation: An Updated Report by the American Society of Anesthesiologists Task Force on Preanesthesia Evaluation

exact_section.heading: Appendix 1 - Preanesthesia history and physical examination

examination.text: Supports record review, patient or family interview, airway examination, prior airway difficulty, OSA symptoms, sleep-study results, and severity documentation.
examination.rationale: The OSA-specific section was reviewed but not mapped because the broad respiratory-risk workflow does not establish suspected or diagnosed OSA.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports record review, patient or family interview, airway examination, prior airway difficulty, OSA symptoms, sleep-study results, and severity documentation.

source.title: Practice Guidelines for the Perioperative Management of Patients with Obstructive Sleep Apnea

exact_section.heading: Preoperative evaluation for suspected or established OSA

source_limitations: Symptoms and duration, exercise tolerance, smoking or vaping, recent infection, asthma, COPD, OSA, prior ventilation, oxygen use, airway, examination, saturation, imaging, pulmonary testing, severity, procedure risk, optimization, technique, monitoring, and postoperative setting remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
